text: I find the texture of a well-worn leather jacket oddly delightful. There's something deeply satisfying about running your hand across leather that's been creased and softened by years of wear—it has this supple, almost velvety quality that develops over time. It's different from the stiff, somewhat plasticky feel of new leather. The way the creases catch light, the slight grain variations, and that subtle give when you press on it all combine into something that just feels honest and lived-in. It's a texture that tells a story and improves with age, which makes it feel less like material and more like a companion object that's settled into comfort alongside you.
